detection_signals: All sweet wines are made with botrytis
If there's no noble rot, the wine can't be sweet
Botrytis is the only way to concentrate wine sugars
why_incorrect: While botrytis is one important method, it is not the only concentration mechanism. Other methods include cryoconcentration, drying, late harvest, fortification.
misconception: The only way to make a concentrated sweet wine is through botrytis (noble rot).
corrected_understanding: Sweet wine concentration can be achieved through multiple methods: botrytis, cryoconcentration, drying, late harvest, fortification, and other dehydration techniques.
